content.text: Every other tree is a body. This one is what the body is carrying.
Systems is the item master, the field medic, the maker, and the summoner — and its power doesn't live on the sheet. It lives in the prep slots. The pre-mission packing decision is the build.
The structural symmetry, printed: the Sin Eater's resource is other people's suffering; Systems' resource is the shopping list. Both are empty alone — one without allies, one without inventory.

content.stat_lean: None — the power lives in the loadout, not a stat. Only Scrapwright names a roll (Tech Check).
content.theme: The loadout class: tuned consumables, both health bars in combat, drones that run on your own AP.
content.design: Structural ceiling: inventory. No supplies, no class — that is the contract.

content.text: Prep slots: 3 at entry, growing with the tree. Consumables and drone chassis loaded into prep slots before a mission are tuned: they cost 1 AP to use instead of the standard 2, and they qualify for this tree's enhancements. The items themselves — medpacks, grenades, repair paste, salvage charges — are statted and priced on Consumables & Ammo.

In combat, Systems tends both health bars: medpacks for flesh, Shell Splint for armor. Conditions belong to the Sin Eater; scars, Saturation, and the long bill belong to a Street Doc and the Medicine skill, in downtime. The medic math is triage, not immortality — enhanced medpacks outpace fodder damage and lose to elites.

Drones are gear: chassis bought or built, carried in prep slots, deployed for 1 AP. They act on your AP — a command costs 1 AP to move or use a function, 2 AP to attack. Drones take no activations of their own; the alternation rhythm is untouched. They are tiny Shells — Value, Absorb, Frame HP — and the full damage pipeline applies (see Attacks & the Shell Clock).

rows: Holds position, grants line of sight, marks (with Spotter Protocol)
Carries one consumable; delivers it on command
2 cargo slots — walks the loot out
Body-blocker: occupies space and counts as an obstacle for cover

content.text: The action tax, printed: an enemy spending its activation killing a 6-Frame drone traded a turn of player-directed violence for a 300-credit toy. Sometimes that's the drone's whole job.

Electronic warfare is gear, not augments: jammer spikes, glare bombs, and intrusion decks live in the Mods catalog (see Mods), and the item master wields them best. One augment below — Overcharged Cells — sharpens them.

The creditor texture: Systems' creditor is the supplier. Every mission ends in a restock bill, and famine — long missions, lost packs, gear-stripping encounters — is the tree's native counter. Scrapwright is the survival valve, not the engine.

content.label: COUNTERPLAY
items: Famine: burn the supplies and the class burns with them.
Drones die to a stiff breeze.
The medic outpaces fodder and loses to elites.
A wrong shopping list — Shell Splints for a Bloom fauna hunt, jammer spikes in the wild — is a build that failed in the shop, not the fight. The intel layer is this class's first roll.

content.text: Requisition manifests are binding once the airlock cycles. Contractors who discover mid-mission that they packed for a different mission are encouraged to reflect on the intel they declined to purchase.
the kit player's nightmare isn't a monster. it's an empty pouch two rooms from the exit.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Drone chassis are gear catalog entries, but no purchase prices are printed in the Phase 7 docs — the action-tax line implies roughly 300 cr for a light chassis, and no catalog confirms or itemizes it.
Tier chassis, requirements, and the install ladder live on Trees Overview. Install costs below are per augment at that tier.

augments.name: Field Harness
augments.effect: 3 prep slots; items in your prep slots are tuned and cost 1 AP to use.

augments.name: Triage Algorithms (Kitbash)
augments.effect: Your medpacks heal +2 and can be administered at Close — launched, slapped, thrown with love. Base medpack stats — see Consumables & Ammo.

augments.name: Cook-Off (Kitbash)
augments.effect: Your grenades apply +1 stack of their rider condition.

augments.name: Spotter Protocol (Marionette)
augments.effect: Your Spotter's mark grants the squad +1 accuracy against the marked target until your next turn.

augments.name: Double-Tap Dispenser (Kitbash)
augments.effect: Medpacks in your prep slots hold 2 uses.

augments.name: Scrapwright (Kitbash)
augments.effect: 2 AP plus a salvage charge: fabricate a one-use Tier-1 consumable mid-mission. Tech Check — full: it works; partial: it works, with a quirk; fail: the scrap is spent. Makeshift gear, formalized.

augments.name: Shell Splint (Kitbash)
augments.effect: 2 AP, touch, consumes repair paste: restore 1d6+2 Armor HP. No permanent-consequence roll.

augments.name: Remote Surgeon (Marionette)
augments.effect: Your Patch-chassis drones deliver your medpacks and Shell Splint at range — your AP, their position. The healer's reach.

augments.name: Overcharged Cells (Kitbash)
augments.effect: Your EW gear — jammer spikes, glare bombs, intrusion decks — forces its contests at disadvantage for the target. The gear itself — see Mods.

augments.name: Best Tool Doctrine (Kitbash)
augments.effect: Once per turn, one prep-slot item costs 0 AP.

augments.name: Swarm Logic (Marionette)
augments.effect: Once per round, one drone command costs 0 AP.

augments.name: Hardened Frames (Marionette)
augments.effect: Your drones gain +1 Value and +4 Frame HP.

augments.name: Walking Depot (Kitbash capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. +3 prep slots, and once per fight use two prep-slot items as a single 2 AP action.

augments.name: Foreman Protocol (Marionette capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Operate 3 active drones, and destroyed drones rebuild free from their wreckage at any rest node.

content.text: Lost, damaged, or improvised equipment remains the financial responsibility of the contractor. The depot restocks at dawn. The depot always restocks at dawn.
